record.title: Emma Christenson Shirrod, 1975-01-21, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Parents were Norwegian homesteaders in the 1870's
record.keywords: wool families death illness homesteads farming women holidays childhood stores Native Americans politics Prohibition dating accidents automobiles winter fires Great Depression colleges and universities

point.title: Preparing sheep wool for use
point.synopsis: Preparing sheep wool for use.

point.title: Christening Emma Shirrod's brother; trip to Jansville
point.synopsis: Christening of Emma Shirrod's brother near Deary. A trip to Jansville to visit the Halseth family.

point.title: Cholera at sea; family background in Wisconsin; children dying of smallpox; boat trip to Walla Walla; mother's family; typhoid fever
point.synopsis: Grandfather's brother died of cholera at sea. Family background in Wisconsin. Father came West in 1876; he could have taken land in Spokane. Two Christenson children died of smallpox at Steptoe. Boat trip to Walla Walla; mother's family. Mother and grandfather take homesteads near Genesee. Mother sold her homestead right to get horses to see fiancée, sick with typhoid fever in Walla Walla.

point.title: Walked over hills to get mail; moving to new Genesee
point.synopsis: Mother said they were too dumb to be afraid during Indian scare (1877-8). Mother walked over hills to get mail. Moving from old to new Genesee.

point.title: Birth in 1885; doctor from Lewiston; uncle with typhoid
point.synopsis: Her birth in 1885 - the doctor came from Lewiston with a team, and also checked uncle with typhoid. Her experience with severe typhoid fever (1905).

point.title: Grandfather's fatal heart attack
point.synopsis: Grandfather's fatal heart attack after a day's work.

point.title: Flour from Colfax or Pomeroy; trip to Lewiston; father's death
point.synopsis: Flour from Colfax or Pomeroy - a year's supply. Trip to Lewiston and back took a day - she wanted to drink from the river. Father's death.

point.title: Brother's death
point.synopsis: Brother's prolonged sickness and death.

point.title: Brother's first sight of gang plow
point.synopsis: Brother's first sight of gang plow.

point.title: Burley stack burned; farming techniques
point.synopsis: Their barley stack burned late in the fall of 1893. Early farming techniques.

point.title: Childhood memories; grandmother's death; candy; Fourth of July
point.synopsis: Early childhood memories: grandmother's death, candy, Fourth of July races.

point.title: Doll from Louis Levi; Genesee stores; local wildflowers
point.synopsis: A "Jew doll" from Louis Levi. Genesee stores and goods. Local wildflowers.

point.title: Kitchen furnishings; helping out when mother was sick
point.synopsis: Kitchen furnishings and utensils. Mother bought one loaf of bread. Helping out; mother was sometimes sick.

point.title: Run-ins with Native Americans; allotment of reservation
point.synopsis: Seeing Indians with their travois in long processions on the trail" run-ins with Indians. Allotment of reservation by Alice Fletcher.

point.title: Native American land
point.synopsis: Ownership pattern of Indian land near her home.

point.title: Ran for county assessor; sentiment for prohibition
point.synopsis: In 1912 she ran for county assessor on the Progressive ticket. Sentiment for prohibition.

point.title: Meeting her husband; moving to Genesee; working; tuberculosis; courtship; Fourth of July
point.synopsis: The saga of meeting her husband: moving to Genesee and working Tuberculosis of Mr. Shirrod's first wife. Emma Shirrod's sojourns in California. Courtship: going to Chautauqua, Fourth of July and camp meeting with Mr. Shirrod.

point.title: Car accident
point.synopsis: A car accident after a premonition.

point.title: Honeymoon on a North Idaho Lake
point.synopsis: Their honeymoon in isolated snow country on a North Idaho lake.

point.title: Disastrous trip to Spokane; broken wrist; father's death
point.synopsis: A presentiment of a disastrous trip to Spokane. Another presentiment before a broken wrist. Crying spells before father died.

point.title: House burnt down; buying another place; the Depression
point.synopsis: Fire burnt down their house in 1925. Their purchase of another place. Success through hard work and savings. The Depression didn't affect them much.

point.title: College in Pullman; spinal trouble; family illnesses
point.synopsis: Taking a business course at the college in Pullman. Her spinal trouble at that time led to a cure in St. Louis. She hurried home when her father became sick. Family illnesses.

point.title: The Shirrod scholarship
point.synopsis: The Shirrod scholarship fund for college-bound students from Genesee.

point.title: Allegiance to Nez Perce County
point.synopsis: Her allegiance to Nez Perce County.

record.description: Family pioneering and childhood experiences. Family illnesses and deaths. Early Genesee. Her courtship. 1-21-75 2 hr
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
